Agent Foundations & Tools.aiRubric: Check agentic concepts, function calling logic, MCP configuration, ReAct loop understanding, and API integration.

lessons.title: Intro to Agentic AI vs Traditional LLMs
lessons.theory:
## Agents vs LLMs
While LLMs just generate text, Agents use tools to take actions in the environment.
```python
# Traditional: model.predict("Hello")
# Agentic: agent.run("Check the weather and email me")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Agent Capability
Assign the string `'Actions'` to the variable describing what Agents can perform that base LLMs cannot.

lessons.title: LLM Tool Calling (Function Calling)
lessons.theory:
## Tool Calling
Models can be provided with a schema of functions to execute.
```python
@tool
def get_weather(location: str):
    return "Sunny"
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Define a Tool
Use the `@tool` decorator on the `search_web` function.

lessons.title: Introduction to the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
lessons.theory:
## MCP
MCP provides a standard interface for agents to access external context and tools securely.
```python
mcp_client = Client(server="filesystem")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: MCP Client
Instantiate an MCP Client connecting to the `'database'` server.

lessons.title: Building a Simple ReAct (Reason + Act) Agent
lessons.theory:
## ReAct Pattern
Agents loop through: Thought, Action, Observation, Answer.
```python
# Thought: I need to search.
# Action: search("MCP")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: ReAct Step
Assign `'Observation'` to the step following an Action.

lessons.title: Connecting Agents to External APIs
lessons.theory:
## API Integration
Agents use tools to fetch data from APIs to ground their responses.
```python
requests.get("https://api.weather.gov")
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Fetch API Data
Write the Python code to perform a `GET` request on `'https://api.example.com'` using the `requests` library.

lessons.title: Core Concepts of Agent Foundations & Tools
lessons.theory: ## Understanding Agent Foundations & Tools\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of Agent Foundations & Tools.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'Agent Foundations & Tools Foundation'.
lessons.solution: def get_concept():\n    return 'Agent Foundations & Tools Foundation'
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'Agent Foundations & Tools Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for Agent Foundations & Tools
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering Agent Foundations & Tools.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to Agent Foundations & Tools.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in Agent Foundations & Tools
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying Agent Foundations & Tools at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure Agent Foundations & Tools projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Create Class\n1. Create a class `Module` with a property `name` initialized to 'Agent Foundations & Tools'.
lessons.solution: class Module:\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.name = 'Agent Foundations & Tools'

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in Agent Foundations & Tools.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in Agent Foundations & Tools.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling Agent Foundations & Tools Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management
lessons.theory: ## Garbage Collection\nUnderstanding how memory is freed is critical.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: GC Status\n1. Set `gc_enabled` to True.

lessons.title: Multi-threading Constructs
lessons.theory: ## Parallelism\nUtilizing multiple cores improves execution speed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Thread Count\n1. Set `threads` to 8.

lessons.title: Final Project: Agent Foundations & Tools
lessons.theory: ## Capstone\nApply all learned concepts to build a comprehensive solution.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Project Initialization\n1. Set `project_name` to 'Agent Foundations & Tools Final'.

Stateful Agents & Memory.aiRubric: Check LangGraph concepts, memory implementations, checkpointing, human-in-the-loop logic, and research agent components.

lessons.title: Intro to LangGraph for Stateful Workflows
lessons.theory:
## LangGraph
A framework for building stateful, multi-actor applications with LLMs.
```python
builder = StateGraph(AgentState)
builder.add_node("reason", reason_node)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Add Node
Add a node named `'act'` mapping to the `action_node` function in the StateGraph.

lessons.title: Implementing Short-Term & Long-Term Memory
lessons.theory:
## Memory
Short-term memory holds the current conversation. Long-term memory uses vector databases.
```python
memory.save_context({"input": "Hi"}, {"output": "Hello"})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Vector DB Type
Assign `'Vector'` to the type of database typically used for Long-Term semantic memory.

lessons.title: Managing Agent State & Checkpointing
lessons.theory:
## Checkpointing
Saves the agent's graph state so workflows can be paused and resumed.
```python
checkpointer = MemorySaver()
graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Compile Graph
Compile the `builder` passing `checkpointer` as an argument.

lessons.title: Human-in-the-Loop (Approval Workflows)
lessons.theory:
## Human-in-the-Loop
Agents pause execution to wait for human approval before dangerous actions.
```python
graph.invoke(state, config={"interrupt_before": ["execute"]})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Interrupt Node
Configure the graph invocation to interrupt before the `'deploy'` node.
lessons.rubric: Graph configured to interrupt before deploy.

lessons.title: Building a Web-Searching Research Agent
lessons.theory:
## Research Agent
A specific agent designed to issue queries, scrape pages, and summarize findings.
```python
@tool
def scrape_page(url: str):
    # logic here
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Research Steps
Assign `'Summarize'` as the final step after querying and scraping.

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Stateful Agents & Memory.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Stateful Agents & Memory.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Stateful Agents & Memory.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

Multi-Agent Orchestration.aiRubric: Check multi-agent network topologies, delegation mechanisms, evaluation harnesses, infinite loop prevention, and deployment concepts.

lessons.title: Multi-Agent Architectures (Hierarchical vs Network)
lessons.theory:
## Topologies
Hierarchical uses a Supervisor agent. Network allows peer-to-peer agent communication.
```python
supervisor.delegate(task, worker_agent)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Supervisor Role
Assign `'Supervisor'` to the architecture where one agent manages others.

lessons.title: Agent Collaboration & Task Delegation
lessons.theory:
## Delegation
Agents pass context and tools to specialized sub-agents.
```python
sub_agent = create_agent(tools=[search])
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Create Sub-Agent
Create a `coder` agent equipped with the `write_code` tool.

lessons.title: Harness Engineering for Agent Evaluation
lessons.theory:
## Evaluation
Agents require automated testing harnesses to evaluate their trajectories and tool usage, not just their final text output.
```python
assert "search_web" in trajectory.tool_calls
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Evaluate Trajectory
Write an assertion checking if `'read_file'` is in `trajectory.tools`.

lessons.title: Handling Agent Failures & Infinite Loops
lessons.theory:
## Max Steps
To prevent infinite ReAct loops, always define a maximum step limit.
```python
graph.invoke(state, {"recursion_limit": 10})
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Recursion Limit
Invoke the graph with a `recursion_limit` of `5`.

lessons.title: Deploying Autonomous Agents to Production
lessons.theory:
## Deployment
Agents in production need streaming, concurrency, and robust error handling. FastAPI and WebSockets are common.
```python
@app.websocket("/ws")
async def agent_stream(ws):
    await ws.send_text(agent_output)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: WebSocket Endpoint
Define a FastAPI websocket endpoint at `"/agent"`.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.title: Logging and Monitoring
lessons.theory: ## Observability\nLogs help diagnose issues in production.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in Multi-Agent Orchestration
lessons.theory: ## Reusable Solutions\nDesign patterns solve common architectural problems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Singleton\n1. Set `pattern` to 'Singleton'.

lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.

lessons.title: Scaling Applications

lessons.instructions: ## Task: Service Name\n1. Set `service` to 'Auth'.
